commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: the first: He is the first, because "by Him were all things made;" the last, because in Him are all things restored. <a

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: I fell: As a man, he trembles at the spiritual vision, but his human fear is banished by the clemency of the Lord.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
I am the first and the last. These are the words of the Son of man, or of him that represented our Saviour, Christ, to St. John. To be the first and the last, is another expression agreeing only to him who is the true God, as it is divers times applied by the prophet Isaias. (Witham)
From the 12th verse to this place we have a description of the Son of man, i.e. Christ. The different emblematical descriptions of his countenance, his dress are similar to what are used by other prophets, and easily explained of his attributes, his eternity, vengeance (Omnes passim.)

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: Something also alludes to the end
